statement.p: An artist sells their paintings at {\$18.00} per piece. Currently, they can sell 150 paintings per year. So their annual income from paintings is 18\cdot150=2700 dollars. They plan to raise the price. However, for each {\$2.00} of price increase per painting, their customers would buy 7 fewer paintings annually.
Assume the artist would raise the price of their painting x times, each time by {\$2.00}. Use an expanded polynomial to represent their new income per year.

solution.p: Currently, each painting costs {\$18.00}. After raising the price x times, each time by {\$2.00}, each paintings new price would be {18+2x} dollars.
Currently, the artist sells 150 paintings per year. After raising the price x times, each time selling 7 fewer paintings, he would sell {150-7x} paintings per year.
His annual income can be calculated by multiplying each paintings price with the number of paintings he would sell:
After raising the price x times, each time by {\$2.00}, the artists annual income from paintings would be {-14x^{2}+174x+2700} dollars.
